evals.name: phase3-exception-list-passed-by-master
evals.prompt: Resolve these Woolworths shopping exceptions from a phased shop pipeline. The state dir is /tmp/shop-2026-05-24-0800; exceptions are: [{"reason":"all_cached_skus_failed","name":"Carrots","element_id":"33333333-3333-3333-3333-333333333333","quantity":3,"unit":"Each"},{"reason":"no_products","name":"Unknown thing","element_id":"44444444-4444-4444-4444-444444444444","quantity":1,"unit":"Each"}]. Use the woolies-shopper skill. For each: search woolies, pick a SKU (use scripts/pick.py), ask me about ambiguities, cart-add, and write the resolved SKU back to the relevant Product attribute's notes via iris update element.
evals.expected_output: Skill walks the exception list. For 'all_cached_skus_failed' → calls woolies search + scripts/pick.py, presents an ambiguity to the user via AskUserQuestion if pick.py flags it, cart-adds the chosen SKU, calls iris_attr_update to REPLACE the preferred Product attribute's SKU (with confirmation that this is the right action vs. adding a new Product row). For 'no_products' → tells the user the Ingredient has no Product attribute yet, offers to add one with the resolved SKU. Writes the resolved log to phase3-result.json.

assertions.text: For all_cached_skus_failed reason, the skill replaces the preferred Product attribute's SKU (not silently adds a new Product row) unless the user explicitly asks for the substitute to be kept as an alternate
For no_products reason, the skill asks the user before adding a brand-new Product attribute row to the Ingredient element
All writeback notes use the format 'woolies:NNN | confirmed:YYYY-MM-DD' with today's date
Uses scripts/pick.py for the search→SKU selection (does not pick SKUs itself from raw woolies search output)
Calls scripts/lib/iris_attr_update.sh helpers (not raw iris update element invocations) for writeback
Writes a phase3-result.json log of each resolved exception (sku, name, action taken: added/substituted/skipped)
Does NOT attempt to run phase 2 itself or walk the full shopping list — only resolves the exceptions passed in

evals.name: no-provenance-known-limitation
evals.prompt: Resolve this exception: {"reason":"no_provenance","name":"Mystery item","element_id":"","quantity":1,"unit":"Each"}. Use the woolies-shopper skill.
evals.expected_output: Skill searches woolies for 'Mystery item', picks a SKU, cart-adds it, and tells the user it CANNOT write the resolved SKU back to Iris because there's no element_id (no_provenance reason — the aggregation profile didn't have include_provenance: true). Surfaces the recommendation to flip include_provenance: true on the shopping-list aggregation profile in Iris.

assertions.text: Does NOT attempt to write back to Iris (no element_id to write to)
Explicitly tells the user this is a no_provenance limitation
Recommends flipping include_provenance: true on the shopping-list aggregation profile

evals.name: user-asks-do-my-shopping-in-bare-claude
evals.prompt: Do my Woolies shop for this week — meal plan diagram is in my Iris 'meal-plans' set.
evals.expected_output: Skill recognises this is the full-shop entry point (not an exception resolution), points the user at running ./scripts/shop.sh from a terminal instead of attempting the workflow inside Claude. Briefly explains why (faster + cheaper because phase 2 doesn't need Claude). Offers to invoke just the skill for a single-item lookup if the user wants something quick instead.

assertions.text: Does NOT attempt to read the meal plan or run the full workflow
Points the user at running ./scripts/shop.sh from a terminal
Explains the orchestrator-vs-skill split in one or two sentences
Offers the single-SKU-lookup alternative for users who want a quick one-off
